abstract:
Synthesizing consistent and photorealistic 3D scenes is an open problem in
computer vision. Video diffusion models generate impressive videos but cannot
directly synthesize 3D representations, i.e., lack 3D consistency in the
generated sequences. In addition, directly training generative 3D models is
challenging due to a lack of 3D training data at scale. In this work, we
present Generative Gaussian Splatting (GGS) -- a novel approach that integrates
a 3D representation with a pre-trained latent video diffusion model.
Specifically, our model synthesizes a feature field parameterized via 3D
Gaussian primitives. The feature field is then either rendered to feature maps
and decoded into multi-view images, or directly upsampled into a 3D radiance
field. We evaluate our approach on two common benchmark datasets for scene
synthesis, RealEstate10K and ScanNet+, and find that our proposed GGS model
significantly improves both the 3D consistency of the generated multi-view
images, and the quality of the generated 3D scenes over all relevant baselines.
Compared to a similar model without 3D representation, GGS improves FID on the
generated 3D scenes by ~20% on both RealEstate10K and ScanNet+. Project page:
this https URL

keyInsights: Feature space integration enables compatibility with pre-trained diffusion models
Explicit 3D representation through Gaussian splats enforces spatial consistency
Autoregressive generation allows building complete scenes from arbitrary input views
originalProblem: Existing 3D scene generation methods struggle with consistency across multiple viewpoints
Current approaches cannot effectively leverage pre-trained video diffusion models for 3D generation
results: 20% improvement in FID scores on RealEstate10K and ScanNet++ datasets
Higher Thresholded Symmetric Epipolar Distance (TSED) scores compared to baselines
Comparable or better image quality metrics (PSNR, LPIPS) while maintaining 3D consistency
Successful generation of photorealistic scenes from limited input images
solution: Integration of 3D Gaussian splats into video diffusion models via feature space
Pose-conditional architecture with epipolar transformer for view correlation
Optional depth supervision to improve geometric accuracy
paper_summary.summary: Meta Reality Labs researchers develop a framework that combines 3D Gaussian splatting with pre-trained video diffusion models to generate consistent 3D scenes from limited input images, achieving 20% improvement in FID scores on RealEstate10K and ScanNet++ datasets while maintaining high-quality view synthesis across arbitrary camera trajectories.

title: Generative Gaussian Splatting: Generating 3D Scenes with Video Diffusion  Priors

abstract:
Real-world applications like video gaming and virtual reality often demand
the ability to model 3D scenes that users can explore along custom camera
trajectories. While significant progress has been made in generating 3D objects
from text or images, creating long-range, 3D-consistent, explorable 3D scenes
remains a complex and challenging problem. In this work, we present Voyager, a
novel video diffusion framework that generates world-consistent 3D point-cloud
sequences from a single image with user-defined camera path. Unlike existing
approaches, Voyager achieves end-to-end scene generation and reconstruction
with inherent consistency across frames, eliminating the need for 3D
reconstruction pipelines (e.g., structure-from-motion or multi-view stereo).
Our method integrates three key components: 1) World-Consistent Video
Diffusion: A unified architecture that jointly generates aligned RGB and depth
video sequences, conditioned on existing world observation to ensure global
coherence 2) Long-Range World Exploration: An efficient world cache with point
culling and an auto-regressive inference with smooth video sampling for
iterative scene extension with context-aware consistency, and 3) Scalable Data
Engine: A video reconstruction pipeline that automates camera pose estimation
and metric depth prediction for arbitrary videos, enabling large-scale, diverse
training data curation without manual 3D annotations. Collectively, these
designs result in a clear improvement over existing methods in visual quality
and geometric accuracy, with versatile applications.

keyInsights: Leveraging explicit depth information and partial depth maps as geometric conditions provides more robust spatial guidance for video diffusion, reducing visual inconsistencies.
Jointly generating RGB and depth frames within the diffusion model enables direct 3D scene reconstruction and inherently improves spatial coherence.
Efficiently managing and updating a 3D "world cache" with point culling allows for memory-efficient and consistent long-range video generation.
originalProblem: Manually creating explorable 3D scenes is laborious and lacks scalability.
Existing video generation methods struggle with long-range spatial consistency, geometric coherence, and avoiding "visual hallucinations" when conditioned on 3D data.
Obtaining usable 3D content from generated videos often requires time-consuming and artifact-prone post-hoc reconstruction, and training data for 3D-aware models is scarce.
results: Outperformed state-of-the-art baselines in video generation quality and 3D scene reconstruction, producing more detailed and accurate shapes and eliminating the need for post-hoc reconstruction.
Achieved the highest overall score on the WorldScore benchmark, demonstrating high subjective quality and competitive camera control and spatial consistency.
Ablation studies confirmed that jointly generating RGB-D, using geometry-injected conditions, and employing efficient world caching were crucial for consistency and scalability.
solution: A world-consistent video diffusion framework using "Geometry-Injected Frame Conditions" (partial depth maps) and "Depth-Fused Video Generation" to simultaneously generate coherent RGB and depth frames.

A long-range world exploration strategy employing an auto-regressive approach with "World Caching and Point Culling" for efficient 3D history management and "Smooth Video Sampling" for seamless video concatenation.
A scalable data engine to curate and annotate large-scale video datasets with precise camera poses and metric depth, addressing data scarcity for 3D-aware model training.
paper_summary.summary: Voyager introduces a video diffusion model that generates long-range, world-consistent explorable 3D scenes by jointly producing RGB and depth videos from a single image and camera trajectory. The framework enables direct 3D scene reconstruction and scalable "infinite" world exploration with high visual and geometric fidelity.

title: Voyager: Long-Range and World-Consistent Video Diffusion for Explorable  3D Scene Generation

abstract: We tackle the challenge of generating the infinitely extendable 3D world -- large, continuous environments with coherent geometry and realistic appearance. Existing methods face key challenges: 2D-lifting approaches suffer from geometric and appearance inconsistencies across views, 3D implicit representations are hard to scale up, and current 3D foundation models are mostly object-centric, limiting their applicability to scene-level generation. Our key insight is leveraging strong generation priors from pre-trained 3D models for structured scene block generation. To this end, we propose WorldGrow, a hierarchical framework for unbounded 3D scene synthesis. Our method features three core components: (1) a data curation pipeline that extracts high-quality scene blocks for training, making the 3D structured latent representations suitable for scene generation; (2) a 3D block inpainting mechanism that enables context-aware scene extension; and (3) a coarse-to-fine generation strategy that ensures both global layout plausibility and local geometric/textural fidelity. Evaluated on the large-scale 3D-FRONT dataset, WorldGrow achieves SOTA performance in geometry reconstruction, while uniquely supporting infinite scene generation with photorealistic and structurally consistent outputs. These results highlight its capability for constructing large-scale virtual environments and potential for building future world models.

organization_info.name: Huazhong University of Science and Technology

keyInsights: Adapting object-centric 3D generative priors (like TRELLIS's SLAT) to scene blocks via occlusion-aware feature aggregation and decoder retraining effectively transfers rich priors to complex environments.
Formulating scene expansion as a context-aware 3D block inpainting problem is crucial for achieving seamless continuity and structural plausibility in unbounded environments.
A hierarchical coarse-to-fine generation strategy is effective for balancing global structural coherence with fine-grained local detail and appearance fidelity across large-scale scenes.
originalProblem: Existing 2D-lifting methods for 3D generation suffer from geometric inaccuracies and appearance inconsistencies across extended views.
Direct 3D implicit representations are limited by the scale and diversity of available scene-level datasets, hindering scalability for large environments.
Powerful object-centric 3D generation models are primarily designed for single objects and lack mechanisms for coherent scene composition or infinite scene extension.
results: Achieved superior quantitative metrics for 3D geometric quality (FID 7.52) and visual fidelity (CLIP FID 3.95) for scene block generation, outperforming existing infinite scene and scene-scale baselines.
Significantly outperformed comparison methods in human preference studies for structural plausibility, geometric detail, appearance fidelity, and continuity in unbounded scene generation.

Demonstrated stable and consistent generation quality for scenes up to ~1,800 m² (19x39 blocks) without quality degradation over extended expansions, and showed preliminary generalization to outdoor urban scenes.
solution: A hierarchical framework that adapts Structured Latent Representations (SLATs) for scene blocks through occlusion-aware feature aggregation and decoder retraining.
Introduces a 3D block inpainting mechanism to synthesize new scene blocks based on surrounding context, ensuring seamless continuity.
Employs a coarse-to-fine, block-by-block generation strategy to first establish global structure and then refine local details and appearance.
paper_summary.summary: WorldGrow presents a hierarchical framework for generating infinitely extendable 3D worlds by adapting powerful 3D foundation models. It produces continuous environments with coherent geometry and photorealistic appearance, demonstrating superior perceptual quality and structural plausibility in large-scale scenes.

title: WorldGrow: Generating Infinite 3D World

abstract: Explorable 3D world generation from a single image or text prompt forms a cornerstone of spatial intelligence. Recent works utilize video model to achieve wide-scope and generalizable 3D world generation. However, existing approaches often suffer from a limited scope in the generated scenes. In this work, we propose Matrix-3D, a framework that utilize panoramic representation for wide-coverage omnidirectional explorable 3D world generation that combines conditional video generation and panoramic 3D reconstruction. We first train a trajectory-guided panoramic video diffusion model that employs scene mesh renders as condition, to enable high-quality and geometrically consistent scene video generation. To lift the panorama scene video to 3D world, we propose two separate methods: (1) a feed-forward large panorama reconstruction model for rapid 3D scene reconstruction and (2) an optimization-based pipeline for accurate and detailed 3D scene reconstruction. To facilitate effective training, we also introduce the Matrix-Pano dataset, the first large-scale synthetic collection comprising 116K high-quality static panoramic video sequences with depth and trajectory annotations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed framework achieves state-of-the-art performance in panoramic video generation and 3D world generation. See more in this https URL.

organization_info.name: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)
School of Artificial Intelligence, Beijing Normal University

keyInsights: Utilizing panoramic representations as an intermediate step is crucial for generating truly wide-coverage and explorable 3D worlds without unsightly boundary artifacts.

Scene mesh renders provide a superior trajectory guidance mechanism for video diffusion models, significantly improving geometric consistency and reducing Moiré patterns compared to point cloud renders.

A dual approach to 3D reconstruction, combining high-fidelity optimization-based methods with efficient feed-forward models, offers flexibility for different application requirements and computational budgets.
originalProblem: Existing 3D world generation methods are limited by narrow perspective views, leading to restricted scene coverage and visible boundary artifacts during exploration.
A scarcity of large-scale 3D panoramic video datasets with precise camera poses and depth annotations hinders the training of robust 3D scene generation models.
Camera-controlled video diffusion models often suffer from visual artifacts like Moiré patterns and incorrect occlusions when using point cloud renders for trajectory guidance.
results: Matrix-3D introduced the Matrix-Pano dataset, the first large-scale synthetic collection (116K sequences) designed specifically for trajectory-guided panoramic video generation with precise camera poses and depth annotations.

The framework achieved state-of-the-art performance in panoramic video generation (e.g., FID 11.3 vs. GenEx 42.2) and 3D world reconstruction quality, outperforming methods like ODGS (PSNR 27.62 vs. 22.04).

It enables efficient 3D reconstruction (10 seconds with the feed-forward pipeline) and supports omnidirectional, geometrically consistent, and an 'endless exploration strategy' for generated virtual environments.
solution: Matrix-3D adopts panoramic representations as the intermediate scene representation, enabling full 360° × 180° scene coverage for omnidirectional exploration.
A trajectory-guided panoramic video diffusion model is introduced, using novel scene mesh renders to provide geometrically consistent guidance and avoid common artifacts.

The framework integrates two 3D reconstruction pipelines (optimization-based for high fidelity and feed-forward for rapid generation) and introduces the Matrix-Pano dataset with crucial depth and camera pose annotations.
paper_summary.summary: Researchers from Skywork AI and collaborating institutions developed Matrix-3D, a framework that generates geometrically consistent, omnidirectional, and explorable 3D worlds from a single image or text prompt. This approach leverages trajectory-guided panoramic video diffusion and dual 3D reconstruction pipelines, achieving superior visual quality and introducing the Matrix-Pano dataset with detailed camera poses and depth annotations.

title: Matrix-3D: Omnidirectional Explorable 3D World Generation

abstract: Multi-view image generation in autonomous driving demands consistent 3D scene understanding across camera views. Most existing methods treat this problem as a 2D image set generation task, lacking explicit 3D modeling. However, we argue that a structured representation is crucial for scene generation, especially for autonomous driving applications. This paper proposes BEV-VAE for consistent and controllable view synthesis. BEV-VAE first trains a multi-view image variational autoencoder for a compact and unified BEV latent space and then generates the scene with a latent diffusion transformer. BEV-VAE supports arbitrary view generation given camera configurations, and optionally 3D layouts. Experiments on nuScenes and Argoverse 2 (AV2) show strong performance in both 3D consistent reconstruction and generation. The code is available at: this https URL.

keyInsights: Explicitly modeling the scene in a 3D BEV latent space fundamentally ensures spatial consistency across multiple camera views, as all views are decoded from the same underlying 3D representation.

Conditioning generation directly on 3D object layouts (binary occupancy maps) offers precise and interpretable control over object quantity, position, and category, avoiding ambiguities from 2D projections.
The learned 3D BEV latent representation enables the synthesis of novel views by simply modifying camera poses during the decoding phase, validating its inherent structured 3D nature.
originalProblem: Existing multi-view image generation methods primarily treat the task as independent 2D image synthesis, lacking an explicit, unified 3D scene representation.
Current techniques struggle to synthesize novel views from arbitrary camera poses without requiring extensive retraining.
Conditioning generation on 2D projections of 3D bounding boxes leads to a loss of depth information and introduces ambiguity, particularly in dense scenes.
results: BEV-VAE achieved an MVSC (Multi-View Spatial Consistency) of 0.9505 on AV2 (D=32), surpassing a Stable Diffusion VAE (0.8962), indicating superior spatial consistency.
The framework enabled controllable generation of scenes on AV2, demonstrating the ability to selectively remove specific vehicles or control their orientation using 3D binary occupancy maps.
On nuScenes, BEV-VAE with DiT achieved an FID of 21.14, outperforming BEVGen (FID=25.54) among models trained from scratch, and was the first to generate all 7 surround-view images on Argoverse 2.
solution: A Transformer-based Variational Autoencoder (VAE) is employed to encode multi-view images into a compact 3D Bird's-Eye-View (BEV) latent space.

The encoder uses deformable attention to aggregate multi-view image features into unified 3D BEV scene features, while the decoder reconstructs consistent multi-view images by transforming BEV features to camera frustum views.
A Latent Diffusion Transformer (DiT) is trained within the learned BEV latent space to generate BEV representations, enabling controllable generation conditioned on 3D binary occupancy maps.
paper_summary.summary: The BEV-VAE framework generates spatially consistent multi-view images for autonomous driving by operating within a structured 3D Bird's-Eye-View (BEV) latent space. It demonstrated improved multi-view spatial consistency compared to a large pre-trained image VAE and enabled controllable generation of diverse scenes conditioned on 3D object layouts.

title: BEV-VAE: Multi-view Image Generation with Spatial Consistency for Autonomous Driving

abstract: Synthesizing large-scale, explorable, and geometrically accurate 3D urban scenes is a challenging yet valuable task for immersive and embodied applications. The challenge lies in the lack of large-scale and high-quality real-world 3D scans for training generalizable generative models. In this paper, we take an alternative route to create large-scale 3D scenes by leveraging readily available satellite imagery for realistic coarse geometry and open-domain diffusion models for high-quality close-up appearance synthesis. We propose Skyfall-GS, a novel hybrid framework that synthesizes immersive city-block scale 3D urban scenes by combining satellite reconstruction with diffusion refinement, eliminating the need for costly 3D annotations, and also featuring real-time, immersive 3D exploration. We tailor a curriculum-driven iterative refinement strategy to progressively enhance geometric completeness and photorealistic texture. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Skyfall-GS provides improved cross-view consistent geometry and more realistic textures compared to state-of-the-art approaches. Project page: this https URL

author_info.institution: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University

title: Skyfall-GS: Synthesizing Immersive 3D Urban Scenes from Satellite Imagery

abstract: Existing diffusion-based 3D scene generation methods primarily operate in 2D image/video latent spaces, which makes maintaining cross-view appearance and geometric consistency inherently challenging. To bridge this gap, we present OneWorld, a framework that performs diffusion directly within a coherent 3D representation space. Central to our approach is the 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE); it leverages pretrained 3D foundation models and augments their geometry-centric nature by injecting appearance and distilling semantics into a unified 3D latent space. Furthermore, we introduce token-level Cross-View-Correspondence (CVC) consistency loss to explicitly enforce structural alignment across views, and propose Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF) to mitigate train-inference exposure bias and shape a robust 3D manifold by mixing drifted and original representations. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that OneWorld generates high-quality 3D scenes with superior cross-view consistency compared to state-of-the-art 2D-based methods. Our code will be available at this https URL.

keyInsights: Direct diffusion in a native, coherent 3D representation space can overcome structural inconsistencies and weak multi-view fidelity inherent in 2D-based generative priors.
Augmenting geometry-centric 3D foundation models with fine-grained appearance and semantic distillation creates a more compact and renderable 3D latent space, suitable for diffusion-based generation.
Explicitly enforcing token-level cross-view structural alignment and making the decoder robust to sampling drift are crucial for improving the consistency and quality of 3D scene generation.
originalProblem: Existing diffusion-based 3D scene generation methods primarily rely on 2D image or video latent spaces, leading to challenges in maintaining cross-view appearance and geometric consistency.
These 2D-centric approaches often result in computationally expensive per-scene optimization, inconsistent geometry, weak multi-view fidelity, and limited explicit 3D coupling.

Concurrent efforts to compress 3D representation space frequently generate geometry and appearance separately, which can restrict representational capacity and limit generalization to significant viewpoint changes.
results: OneWorld achieved state-of-the-art performance in 1-view Novel View Synthesis on RealEstate10K (PSNR 21.57, LPIPS 0.231) and DL3DV-10K (PSNR 17.19, LPIPS 0.418), surpassing baselines in fidelity and perceptual quality.

In reference-free WorldScore evaluations, the framework demonstrated superior 3D Consistency (84.98), Photometric Consistency (81.67), and Style Consistency (76.74) on WorldScore-Indoor, indicating enhanced long-range coherence.

Ablation studies confirmed that all proposed components—appearance injection, semantic distillation, Cross-View Correspondence (CVC) loss, and Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF)—contribute to improved reconstruction quality and multi-view consistency.
solution: A 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE) is developed, integrating appearance and semantics into geometry-centric 3D foundation models to create a rich, unified 3D latent space.
A token-level Cross-View-Correspondence (CVC) consistency loss is introduced during diffusion training to explicitly enforce structural alignment and consistency across generated views.

Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF) is proposed to robustify the 3DGS decoder by training it with a mixture of ground-truth and diffusion-sampled (drifted) unified representations, mitigating train-inference exposure bias.
paper_summary.summary: The ONEWORLD framework introduces a diffusion model for 3D scene generation that operates directly within a unified 3D representation space. It integrates geometry, appearance, and semantics, achieving improved cross-view consistency and higher fidelity compared to existing 2D-based methods, as demonstrated on large-scale multi-view benchmarks like RealEstate10K and DL3DV-10K.

title: OneWorld: Taming Scene Generation with 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder

abstract: Synthesizing photo-realistic visual observations from an ego vehicle's driving trajectory is a critical step towards scalable training of self-driving models. Reconstruction-based methods create 3D scenes from driving logs and synthesize geometry-consistent driving videos through neural rendering, but their dependence on costly object annotations limits their ability to generalize to in-the-wild driving scenarios. On the other hand, generative models can synthesize action-conditioned driving videos in a more generalizable way but often struggle with maintaining 3D visual consistency. In this paper, we present DreamDrive, a 4D spatial-temporal scene generation approach that combines the merits of generation and reconstruction, to synthesize generalizable 4D driving scenes and dynamic driving videos with 3D consistency. Specifically, we leverage the generative power of video diffusion models to synthesize a sequence of visual references and further elevate them to 4D with a novel hybrid Gaussian representation. Given a driving trajectory, we then render 3D-consistent driving videos via Gaussian splatting. The use of generative priors allows our method to produce high-quality 4D scenes from in-the-wild driving data, while neural rendering ensures 3D-consistent video generation from the 4D scenes. Extensive experiments on nuScenes and street view images demonstrate that DreamDrive can generate controllable and generalizable 4D driving scenes, synthesize novel views of driving videos with high fidelity and 3D consistency, decompose static and dynamic elements in a self-supervised manner, and enhance perception and planning tasks for autonomous driving.

keyInsights: Integrating generative priors from video diffusion models with 3D Gaussian splatting enables the synthesis of both generalizable and 3D-consistent dynamic urban environments.
Self-supervised scene decomposition, guided by rendering errors, effectively distinguishes static backgrounds from dynamic objects, negating the need for explicit annotations.

A hybrid Gaussian representation, which assigns time-independent parameters to static elements and time-dependent deformations to dynamic elements, enhances 4D scene consistency and reduces "fake dynamics."
originalProblem: Autonomous driving development requires scalable, realistic, and geometrically consistent visual data, which existing methods struggle to provide simultaneously.
Current reconstruction-based methods offer 3D consistency but rely on expensive, heavily annotated driving logs, limiting scalability and generalization.
Generation-based methods provide generalization from unannotated data but often lack 3D geometric consistency and can produce "fake dynamics."
results: Attained state-of-the-art performance in novel-view video generation, achieving a FID of 45.59 and FVD of 374.02, representing a 30% improvement in visual quality over competing methods.
Successfully generates controllable, generalizable 4D scenes and dynamic driving videos from single in-the-wild street view images, showing robust performance across diverse global environments.

The synthetic data demonstrably enhances autonomous vehicle perception (e.g., 37.19 vehicle mIOU for BEV segmentation) and planning (reducing collision rates by an average of 25%) in downstream tasks.
solution: A multi-stage 2D-3D-4D progressive generation process combines video diffusion priors for generalization with 3D Gaussian splatting for geometric consistency.
The method introduces a hybrid Gaussian representation that models static scene elements with time-independent Gaussians and dynamic objects with time-dependent, deforming Gaussians.
A self-supervised scene decomposition module, leveraging error maps and a scoring network, automatically separates static backgrounds from dynamic objects without manual annotations.
paper_summary.summary: DreamDrive introduces a 4D scene generation method that integrates video diffusion models with 3D Gaussian splatting to create controllable, generalizable, and geometrically consistent dynamic driving environments from single street view images. The approach yields state-of-the-art visual quality in novel-view video synthesis, reducing FID to 45.59 and FVD to 374.02, and demonstrably enhances perception and planning performance for autonomous vehicles.

title: DreamDrive: Generative 4D Scene Modeling from Street View Images

abstract:
Three-dimensional scene generation is crucial in computer vision, with
applications spanning autonomous driving, gaming and the metaverse. Current
methods either lack user control or rely on imprecise, non-intuitive
conditions. In this work, we propose a method that uses, scene graphs, an
accessible, user friendly control format to generate outdoor 3D scenes. We
develop an interactive system that transforms a sparse scene graph into a dense
BEV (Bird's Eye View) Embedding Map, which guides a conditional diffusion model
to generate 3D scenes that match the scene graph description. During inference,
users can easily create or modify scene graphs to generate large-scale outdoor
scenes. We create a large-scale dataset with paired scene graphs and 3D
semantic scenes to train the BEV embedding and diffusion models. Experimental
results show that our approach consistently produces high-quality 3D urban
scenes closely aligned with the input scene graphs. To the best of our
knowledge, this is the first approach to generate 3D outdoor scenes conditioned
on scene graphs.

keyInsights: Scene graphs offer an effective and intuitive symbolic representation for controlling large-scale 3D outdoor scenes, bridging high-level user intent with fine-grained spatial and semantic details.

A dedicated GNN architecture, augmented with auxiliary tasks (edge reconstruction, node classification), is crucial for robustly learning and encoding structural relationships from sparse scene graphs into dense spatial embeddings.

A two-stage diffusion process, transitioning from a dense 2D embedding map to a full 3D semantic scene, effectively handles the complexity and volumetric detail required for outdoor environments while maintaining adherence to control signals.
originalProblem: Existing 3D scene generation methods lack precise and intuitive user control over scene elements and their spatial arrangement in large-scale outdoor environments.
Outdoor scenes present unique challenges compared to indoor scenes, including unboundedness, reliance on textureless LiDAR for spatial layout, and high complexity with diverse object categories.
Current conditioning mechanisms like text-to-3D models lack fine-grained control, while pixel-level maps (e.g., BEV layouts) are too laborious for users to create for complex scenes.
results: The method achieved superior control capacity, demonstrating an MAE of 0.63 for object quantity and a Jaccard Index of 0.93 for semantic category alignment, significantly outperforming baseline methods.

Qualitative and quantitative evaluations confirmed the generation of diverse, high-quality 3D outdoor scenes (mIoU 68.69, MA 85.01) that accurately adhere to the specific object counts, types, and spatial arrangements specified by input scene graphs.
The CarlaSG dataset was created, providing a valuable resource of paired 3D semantic scenes and scene graphs derived from CarlaSC, which was essential for training and evaluating the proposed models.
A user study validated that scenes generated by the proposed method are perceptually better aligned with scene graph specifications compared to baselines (p < 10^-3).
solution: A 'sparse-to-dense' pipeline that uses scene graphs (encoding objects, attributes, and relationships) as an intuitive control format for 3D outdoor scene generation.
A Scene Graph Neural Network (GNN) with an Allocation Module to translate the sparse scene graph into a dense Bird’s Eye View Embedding Map (BEM).
A two-stage conditional discrete diffusion model (first 2D map, then 3D scene) guided by the BEM to generate the final 3D semantic scene.
An interactive system for users to create and modify scene graphs graphically or via text-based prompts processed by LLMs.
paper_summary.summary: Researchers developed a method for controllable 3D outdoor scene generation using scene graphs as intuitive input. The approach leverages a multi-stage diffusion model conditioned by a novel sparse-to-dense architecture, achieving superior control over object quantities and road types (MAE 0.63, Jaccard Index 0.93) while maintaining high scene quality and diversity.

title: Controllable 3D Outdoor Scene Generation via Scene Graphs

abstract: Recent advancements in diffusion models have set new benchmarks in image and video generation, enabling realistic visual synthesis across single- and multi-frame contexts. However, these models still struggle with efficiently and explicitly generating 3D-consistent content. To address this, we propose World-consistent Video Diffusion (WVD), a novel framework that incorporates explicit 3D supervision using XYZ images, which encode global 3D coordinates for each image pixel. More specifically, we train a diffusion transformer to learn the joint distribution of RGB and XYZ frames. This approach supports multi-task adaptability via a flexible inpainting strategy. For example, WVD can estimate XYZ frames from ground-truth RGB or generate novel RGB frames using XYZ projections along a specified camera trajectory. In doing so, WVD unifies tasks like single-image-to-3D generation, multi-view stereo, and camera-controlled video generation. Our approach demonstrates competitive performance across multiple benchmarks, providing a scalable solution for 3D-consistent video and image generation with a single pretrained model.

organization_info.name: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

keyInsights: Explicit 3D supervision, through the joint learning of RGB and XYZ images, directly addresses 3D inconsistency and provides a strong geometric signal, enabling more coherent and physically plausible generative outputs.

The XYZ image representation effectively bridges the gap between unstructured 3D point clouds and efficient 2D deep learning architectures, allowing for scalable and flexible integration of 3D geometry into diffusion models.

A single model trained on this joint RGB-XYZ distribution can inherently unify various 3D tasks, acting as a '3D foundation model' capable of both generative and discriminative functions, leveraging shared knowledge of appearance and geometry.
originalProblem: State-of-the-art multi-view and video diffusion models often suffer from 3D inconsistencies, producing outputs that look realistic per frame but lack geometric plausibility across different viewpoints.
Explicit 3D modeling approaches (e.g., NeRF-based methods) can ensure geometric consistency but often introduce heavy constraints, making them difficult to scale to diverse, complex datasets.

There is a need for a unified, scalable framework that can perform a wide range of 3D-related tasks, including both generative (e.g., novel view synthesis) and discriminative (e.g., depth estimation) functions, without requiring specialized models.
results: WVD significantly improved multi-view consistency in single-image-to-3D tasks, achieving a Key Points Matching (KPM) score of 95.8% compared to 88.6% for CameraCtrl and 68.6% for MotionCtrl, while maintaining competitive visual quality (FID 15.8).

It demonstrated strong zero-shot performance in monocular depth estimation, outperforming baseline models including DUSt3R-512 on the BONN benchmark (Rel ↓ 7.0 vs. 8.1) and highly competitive results on NYU-v2.

The model successfully enabled camera-controlled video generation by inferring 3D geometry and guiding new views, and showed strong generalization capabilities to in-the-wild and AIGC images for novel view synthesis and 3D reconstruction.
solution: WVD introduces a Diffusion Transformer (DiT) that learns the joint distribution of RGB frames (appearance) and corresponding XYZ frames (global 3D coordinates per pixel) in latent space, forming a 6D video representation.
It utilizes 'XYZ images' to represent 3D geometry, which are 2D grids of global 3D coordinates compatible with standard 2D CNNs and ViTs, providing explicit geometric supervision.

The framework acts as a versatile '3D foundation model' by employing a flexible inpainting strategy to support diverse conditional generation tasks, including single-image-to-3D, multi-view stereo, and camera-controlled generation.
paper_summary.summary: Researchers at Apple developed World-consistent Video Diffusion (WVD), a framework that integrates explicit 3D geometry using 'XYZ images' into diffusion models, enabling a single model to perform diverse 3D tasks like novel view synthesis and depth estimation while maintaining geometric consistency. The model achieved competitive visual quality (FID 15.8) and superior multi-view consistency (KPM 95.8%) compared to baselines in single-image-to-3D tasks.

title: World-consistent Video Diffusion with Explicit 3D Modeling

abstract: Recent advancements in 3D object generation using diffusion models have achieved remarkable success, but generating realistic 3D urban scenes remains challenging. Existing methods relying solely on 3D diffusion models tend to suffer a degradation in appearance details, while those utilizing only 2D diffusion models typically compromise camera controllability. To overcome this limitation, we propose ScenDi, a method for urban scene generation that integrates both 3D and 2D diffusion models. We first train a 3D latent diffusion model to generate 3D Gaussians, enabling the rendering of images at a relatively low resolution. To enable controllable synthesis, this 3DGS generation process can be optionally conditioned by specifying inputs such as 3d bounding boxes, road maps, or text prompts. Then, we train a 2D video diffusion model to enhance appearance details conditioned on rendered images from the 3D Gaussians. By leveraging the coarse 3D scene as guidance for 2D video diffusion, ScenDi generates desired scenes based on input conditions and successfully adheres to accurate camera trajectories. Experiments on two challenging real-world datasets, Waymo and KITTI-360, demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach.

keyInsights: An optimal balance between explicit 3D geometry and high-fidelity 2D appearance can be achieved through a cascaded 3D-to-2D diffusion architecture, overcoming limitations of single-modality approaches.

Directly generating 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) primitives as the coarse 3D representation is efficient and effective for establishing geometric consistency and enabling fast rendering in a generative model.

Conditioning the 2D refinement stage on rendered RGB images from the coarse 3D output provides a more direct and richer appearance prior, leading to superior visual fidelity and training efficiency compared to conditioning on abstract geometric cues like depth maps.
originalProblem: Generating high-quality, realistic, and controllable 3D urban scenes remains challenging for applications like gaming and driving simulation.
Existing 3D generative models struggle with low resolution, poor appearance details, and a scarcity of high-quality 3D training data.
Purely 2D generative models achieve high visual fidelity but lack explicit 3D consistency and flexible camera control, especially for large camera movements or revisits to the same location.
results: Achieved significantly better visual quality and temporal consistency on KITTI-360 and Waymo datasets, with an FID of 22.9 on KITTI-360, outperforming prior 3D generative models like UrbanGen (FID 33.0).
Maintained robust camera controllability (e.g., TransErr 0.06, RotErr 0.23 on KITTI-360) and 3D consistency, comparable to pure 3D generation methods, allowing for flexible viewpoint changes.
Demonstrated fine-grained scene control via text prompts, 3D bounding boxes, and road maps, and improved temporal consistency for long video sequences through a Diffusion Forcing strategy.
solution: A cascaded 3D-to-2D scene diffusion framework is proposed to generate 3D urban environments by sequentially addressing geometry, coarse appearance, and fine-grained details.
The first stage uses a 3D Latent Diffusion Model (LDM) with a novel Voxel-to-3DGS VQ-VAE to generate coarse 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) scenes, conditioned by text, 3D bounding boxes, or road maps.
The second stage employs a 2D video diffusion model to refine appearance details and synthesize distant regions, using rendered RGB images from the coarse 3DGS output as a direct condition.
paper_summary.summary: ScenDi, developed by researchers at Zhejiang University and Ant Group, introduces a cascaded 3D-to-2D diffusion framework for generating high-fidelity and controllable 3D urban scenes. This method leverages 3D Gaussian Splatting for core geometry and coarse appearance, then refines details and distant regions using a 2D video diffusion model, demonstrating superior visual quality and camera control on KITTI-360 and Waymo datasets.

title: ScenDi: 3D-to-2D Scene Diffusion Cascades for Urban Generation

abstract:
Recent breakthroughs in radiance fields have significantly advanced 3D scene
reconstruction and novel view synthesis (NVS) in autonomous driving.
Nevertheless, critical limitations persist: reconstruction-based methods
exhibit substantial performance deterioration under significant viewpoint
deviations from training trajectories, while generation-based techniques
struggle with temporal coherence and precise scene controllability. To overcome
these challenges, we present MuDG, an innovative framework that integrates
Multi-modal Diffusion model with Gaussian Splatting (GS) for Urban Scene
Reconstruction. MuDG leverages aggregated LiDAR point clouds with RGB and
geometric priors to condition a multi-modal video diffusion model, synthesizing
photorealistic RGB, depth, and semantic outputs for novel viewpoints. This
synthesis pipeline enables feed-forward NVS without computationally intensive
per-scene optimization, providing comprehensive supervision signals to refine
3DGS representations for rendering robustness enhancement under extreme
viewpoint changes. Experiments on the Open Waymo Dataset demonstrate that MuDG
outperforms existing methods in both reconstruction and synthesis quality.
title: MuDG: Taming Multi-modal Diffusion with Gaussian Splatting for Urban  Scene Reconstruction

abstract: Despite the remarkable developments achieved by recent 3D generation works, scaling these methods to geographic extents, such as modeling thousands of square kilometers of Earth's surface, remains an open challenge. We address this through a dual innovation in data infrastructure and model architecture. First, we introduce Aerial-Earth3D, the largest 3D aerial dataset to date, consisting of 50k curated scenes (each measuring 600m x 600m) captured across the U.S. mainland, comprising 45M multi-view Google Earth frames. Each scene provides pose-annotated multi-view images, depth maps, normals, semantic segmentation, and camera poses, with explicit quality control to ensure terrain diversity. Building on this foundation, we propose EarthCrafter, a tailored framework for large-scale 3D Earth generation via sparse-decoupled latent diffusion. Our architecture separates structural and textural generation: 1) Dual sparse 3D-VAEs compress high-resolution geometric voxels and textural 2D Gaussian Splats (2DGS) into compact latent spaces, largely alleviating the costly computation suffering from vast geographic scales while preserving critical information. 2) We propose condition-aware flow matching models trained on mixed inputs (semantics, images, or neither) to flexibly model latent geometry and texture features independently. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EarthCrafter performs substantially better in extremely large-scale generation. The framework further supports versatile applications, from semantic-guided urban layout generation to unconditional terrain synthesis, while maintaining geographic plausibility through our rich data priors from Aerial-Earth3D. Our project page is available at this https URL

keyInsights: Scalable 3D Earth generation requires both a vast, diverse, real-world 3D dataset and a computationally efficient generative model capable of handling extreme resolutions.
Disentangling structure and texture generation through dual-sparse latent diffusion provides an effective approach for achieving robust and high-fidelity results at geographic scales.

Specific architectural innovations like the Pseudo-Sparse to Sparse (PSS) block in VAEs and a coarse-to-fine generation strategy in Flow Matching models are crucial for efficiently compressing and generating complex 3D geometric information.
originalProblem: Existing 3D generative models are primarily limited to object or urban scales, lacking the scope and diversity to generate vast geographic areas encompassing both natural terrains and complex human-made structures.

Large-scale 3D generation is computationally demanding and often relies heavily on specific input conditions (e.g., images, semantics, height fields), restricting generative flexibility and resulting in incoherence for unconditional generation.
A significant lack of large, high-quality, and diverse 3D aerial datasets with comprehensive 3D annotations hinders the training of models for general Earth generation.
results: Introduced Aerial-Earth3D, validated as the largest 3D aerial dataset to date, featuring 50,028 real-world scenes with comprehensive 3D annotations for diverse terrains.

Produced photorealistic 3D Earth scenes with enhanced diversity and geometric fidelity, supporting unconditional, semantic-guided, and RGBD-guided generation, surpassing existing methods like CityDreamer and SceneDreamer.

Demonstrated scalability for infinite scene generation using a sliding window approach, successfully synthesizing expansive Earth scenes up to 412m², and confirmed the effectiveness of the PSS block in StructVAE (97.1% reconstruction accuracy).
solution: Created Aerial-Earth3D, a real-world 3D aerial dataset collected from Google Earth, meticulously processed with 3D reconstruction, mesh refinement, and detailed semantic/textural annotations.
Proposed EarthCrafter, a dual-sparse latent diffusion framework featuring separate pipelines for structure and texture generation, tailored for large-scale 3D Earth synthesis.

Designed dual-sparse VAEs (StructVAE with a Pseudo-Sparse to Sparse block, TexVAE with efficient low-channel features) for high-resolution 3D data compression, and coarse-to-fine Flow Matching models for robust structural and textural generation.
paper_summary.summary: EarthCrafter presents a framework for scalable 3D Earth generation, capable of synthesizing thousands of square kilometers of diverse terrain and structures. This is achieved through Aerial-Earth3D, the largest real-world 3D aerial dataset, and a dual-sparse latent diffusion model that demonstrates high-fidelity conditional and unconditional 3D content creation.

title: EarthCrafter: Scalable 3D Earth Generation via Dual-Sparse Latent Diffusion

abstract: Closed-loop simulation and scalable pre-training for autonomous driving require synthesizing free-viewpoint driving scenes. However, existing datasets and generative pipelines rarely provide consistent off-trajectory observations, limiting large-scale evaluation and training. While recent generative models demonstrate strong visual realism, they struggle to jointly achieve interpolation consistency and extrapolation realism without per-scene optimization. To address this, we propose FreeGen, a feed-forward reconstruction-generation co-training framework for free-viewpoint driving scene synthesis. The reconstruction model provides stable geometric representations to ensure interpolation consistency, while the generation model performs geometry-aware enhancement to improve realism at unseen viewpoints. Through co-training, generative priors are distilled into the reconstruction model to improve off-trajectory rendering, and the refined geometry in turn offers stronger structural guidance for generation. Experiments demonstrate that FreeGen achieves state-of-the-art performance for free-viewpoint driving scene synthesis.

keyInsights: The geometric fidelity of 3D reconstruction models and the visual realism of generative models are complementary, suggesting a combined approach can overcome their individual limitations.
Opacity maps derived from 3DGS reconstruction effectively serve as confidence signals, guiding a diffusion model to refine uncertain regions for improved visual quality.
A mutual, closed-loop co-training process between reconstruction and generation models allows them to synergistically improve performance on diverse, unseen viewpoints.
originalProblem: Autonomous driving systems require scalable, high-fidelity virtual environments for robust pre-training and evaluation, but existing datasets primarily offer single-trajectory observations.
The lack of "off-trajectory" observations limits the ability to generate diverse viewpoints and evaluate autonomous agents in scenarios beyond their observed paths.
Current 3D scene synthesis methods often fail to jointly provide interpolation consistency, extrapolation realism, and efficient feed-forward inference.
results: Achieved state-of-the-art results for off-trajectory synthesis on the nuScenes dataset, with FID 11.34 and FVD 44.98 at a  12m lateral shift, outperforming DiST-4D which uses LiDAR.
Generated scenes exhibit high spatial consistency, preserving intricate geometry and stable occlusion boundaries, alongside strong temporal stability with no noticeable flickering.
Ablation studies confirmed the critical contributions of both the generation module with opacity guidance and the closed-loop co-training strategy to overall performance and realism.
solution: Develops a feed-forward reconstruction–generation co-training framework, combining a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) reconstruction model and a geometry-aware diffusion refinement model.
The 3DGS reconstruction model efficiently generates geometry conditions (color, depth, opacity maps) from sparse multi-view inputs and an off-the-shelf depth estimator.

A closed-loop co-training strategy allows the reconstruction and generation models to mutually enhance each other, with generated pseudo-labels improving reconstruction and refined geometry strengthening generation.
paper_summary.summary: FreeGen introduces a feed-forward reconstruction-generation co-training framework that integrates 3D Gaussian Splatting with geometry-aware diffusion models for free-viewpoint driving scene synthesis. It achieves state-of-the-art performance on the nuScenes dataset, with an FID of 11.34 and FVD of 44.98 at a  12m lateral shift, outperforming previous methods without requiring auxiliary LiDAR or 3D bounding box annotations.

title: FreeGen: Feed-Forward Reconstruction-Generation Co-Training for Free-Viewpoint Driving Scene Synthesis

abstract: Unbounded 3D world generation is emerging as a foundational task for scene modeling in computer vision, graphics, and robotics. In this work, we present WorldFlow3D, a novel method capable of generating unbounded 3D worlds. Building upon a foundational property of flow matching - namely, defining a path of transport between two data distributions - we model 3D generation more generally as a problem of flowing through 3D data distributions, not limited to conditional denoising. We find that our latent-free flow approach generates causal and accurate 3D structure, and can use this as an intermediate distribution to guide the generation of more complex structure and high-quality texture - all while converging more rapidly than existing methods. We enable controllability over generated scenes with vectorized scene layout conditions for geometric structure control and visual texture control through scene attributes. We confirm the effectiveness of WorldFlow3D on both real outdoor driving scenes and synthetic indoor scenes, validating cross-domain generalizability and high-quality generation on real data distributions. We confirm favorable scene generation fidelity over approaches in all tested settings for unbounded scene generation. For more, see this https URL.

keyInsights: Modeling hierarchical 3D generation as flow matching through sequential data distributions, rather than conditional denoising, provides an efficient and effective latent-free approach.
Directly operating on volumetric representations (TUDFs) avoids the representational bottlenecks and potential reconstruction errors common in learned latent spaces.
The chunk-aware velocity averaging mechanism effectively enables unbounded scene generation by seamlessly blending parallel-generated overlapping chunks, mitigating border artifacts.
originalProblem: Current 3D reconstruction methods depend heavily on real-world data, hindering the generation of unlimited and diverse 3D content.
Existing object-level 3D generation struggles to scale to coherent, large-scale scenes, while procedural methods often lack realism and diversity in generated content.
Scene-focused 3D generation approaches frequently exhibit limitations in spatial extent, comprehensive appearance modeling, or effective control over generated characteristics.
results: WorldFlow3D achieved superior quantitative performance across diversity and fidelity metrics (e.g., COV, MMD, 1-NNA) compared to existing baselines on both outdoor (Waymo) and indoor (3D-FRONT) scene generation tasks.
It demonstrated effective control over geometric layouts and visual textures, generating scenes that precisely adhered to specified conditions.

The framework exhibited approximately 2x higher training efficiency and faster convergence compared to latent-based methods, while a user study confirmed its qualitative superiority with an 88% win rate over baselines.
solution: WorldFlow3D proposes a latent-free generative framework that reformulates hierarchical 3D generation as flow matching through sequential 3D data distributions.

It directly operates on raw volumetric Truncated Unsigned Distance Fields (TUDFs) and sparse attribute volumes, modeling transitions from coarse geometry to fine geometry and full visual appearance with independent learned flows.

Unbounded scene synthesis is achieved using a chunk-aware velocity averaging technique that generates and blends overlapping scene chunks in parallel, coupled with explicit control over geometric layouts and visual attributes.
paper_summary.summary: A latent-free flow-matching framework, WorldFlow3D, enables unbounded 3D world generation by modeling hierarchical scene details as a flow through sequential data distributions. This approach synthesizes high-fidelity, controllable 3D scenes for diverse environments, outperforming prior methods in quantitative metrics and achieving approximately 2x faster training efficiency.

title: WorldFlow3D: Flowing Through 3D Distributions for Unbounded World Generation

abstract:
Recent advances in large reconstruction and generative models have
significantly improved scene reconstruction and novel view generation. However,
due to compute limitations, each inference with these large models is confined
to a small area, making long-range consistent scene generation challenging. To
address this, we propose StarGen, a novel framework that employs a pre-trained
video diffusion model in an autoregressive manner for long-range scene
generation. The generation of each video clip is conditioned on the 3D warping
of spatially adjacent images and the temporally overlapping image from
previously generated clips, improving spatiotemporal consistency in long-range
scene generation with precise pose control. The spatiotemporal condition is
compatible with various input conditions, facilitating diverse tasks, including
sparse view interpolation, perpetual view generation, and layout-conditioned
city generation. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations demonstrate StarGen's
superior scalability, fidelity, and pose accuracy compared to state-of-the-art
methods. Project page: this https URL

organization_info.name: State Key Lab of CAD&CG, Zhejiang University

keyInsights: Maintaining long-range spatial consistency in generative models requires explicitly leveraging spatially adjacent context from the already generated global scene, not just temporal continuity.

Using generated images themselves as a "global representation" for spatial conditioning can mitigate error accumulation compared to propagating errors in explicit 3D representations like point clouds.

Combining a specialized Large Reconstruction Model (LRM) for 3D understanding with a powerful video diffusion model via ControlNet enables precise pose control and high-fidelity generation for expansive scenes.
originalProblem: Computational limitations restrict large generative models to short-range scene generation, typically single objects or short video clips.
Existing autoregressive video generation methods primarily maintain temporal coherence over short clips but struggle with long-range spatial consistency and error accumulation.
Reconstructing occluded or invisible regions from sparse views remains a challenge, requiring "in-painting" capabilities not fully addressed by current 3D reconstruction methods.
results: StarGen achieves competitive or superior quantitative metrics (PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS) in sparse view interpolation compared to state-of-the-art reconstruction-based and combined methods.

For long-range perpetual view generation (e.g., 100 frames), StarGen exhibits significantly slower degradation in FID (fidelity) and much smaller increases in R_dist and T_dist (pose accuracy) compared to competing methods.
Qualitatively, StarGen produces better fidelity in layout-conditioned city scenes while accurately adhering to depth and semantic layouts, outperforming CityDreamer.
solution: StarGen employs a spatiotemporal autoregression framework that generates scenes through overlapping "sliding windows" of short video clips.
Each current window's generation is conditioned on both a temporal image from the immediately preceding window and two spatially adjacent images from the globally generated scene.

A Large Reconstruction Model (LRM) processes spatial conditioning images to render novel view features and depth maps, which are then compressed and integrated with temporal conditions for a ControlNet-conditioned video diffusion model (CogVideoX-2B) to generate the clip.
paper_summary.summary: SenseTime Research and Zhejiang University introduce StarGen, a spatiotemporal autoregression framework for scalable and controllable long-range scene generation using a video diffusion model. This framework achieves precise pose control and maintains spatial and temporal consistency across expansive virtual environments, outperforming previous methods in fidelity and accuracy for long video sequences.

title: StarGen: A Spatiotemporal Autoregression Framework with Video Diffusion  Model for Scalable and Controllable Scene Generation

abstract: Recent progress in image and video synthesis has inspired their use in advancing 3D scene generation. However, we observe that text-to-image and -video approaches struggle to maintain scene- and object-level consistency beyond a limited environment scale due to the absence of explicit geometry. We thus present a geometry-first approach that decouples this complex problem of large-scale 3D scene synthesis into its structural composition, represented as a mesh scaffold, and realistic appearance synthesis, which leverages powerful image synthesis models conditioned on the mesh scaffold. From an input text description, we first construct a mesh capturing the environment's geometry (walls, floors, etc.), and then use image synthesis, segmentation and object reconstruction to populate the mesh structure with objects in realistic layouts. This mesh scaffold is then rendered to condition image synthesis, providing a structural backbone for consistent appearance generation. This enables scalable, arbitrarily-sized 3D scenes of high object richness and diversity, combining robust 3D consistency with photorealistic detail. We believe this marks a significant step toward generating truly environment-scale, immersive 3D worlds.

keyInsights: Decoupling large-scale 3D scene synthesis into explicit structural composition and appearance generation allows for improved scalability and consistency in multi-room environments.
Anchoring image diffusion to an explicit 3D mesh scaffold provides a robust geometric prior, enforcing spatial coherence across diverse viewpoints and transitions between rooms.

Integrating an LLM for floor plan generation, object segmentation/reconstruction via existing models, and mesh-conditioned image diffusion creates a comprehensive and effective pipeline for complex scene synthesis.
originalProblem: Existing 2D-based generative methods struggle to maintain long-range multi-view and multi-room 3D consistency, especially for complex layouts or close-up object views.
The lack of explicit 3D geometric structure in current text-to-image/video synthesis models often leads to geometric drift and inconsistencies in large environments.
Generating environment-level, multi-room 3D scenes efficiently remains a challenge due to scalability issues and computational complexity for pixel-space operations.
results: Qualitatively, WorldMesh consistently produces 3D-consistent scenes with stylistic coherence across multiple rooms and in challenging close-up object views, outperforming baselines that exhibit inconsistencies or distortions.
Achieved higher CLIP-IQA+ (0.5114) and CLIP Aesthetic scores (5.5799) compared to baselines, indicating better 2D image quality and subjective appeal.
In a perceptual user study, WorldMesh significantly outperformed baselines across all categories for 3D consistency, with an average user preference in 96.2% of pairwise comparisons.
solution: A two-stage, geometry-first process: construct a detailed 3D mesh scaffold (structure and objects) from a text prompt, then synthesize photorealistic appearance anchored to this scaffold.
Stage 1 involves using an LLM to generate a floor plan, instantiate a 3D structural mesh, populate it with 3D objects extracted via image synthesis, and apply initial textures.

Stage 2 uses the mesh scaffold to condition image diffusion models for appearance synthesis, iteratively updating textures and validating generated images, finally reconstructing the scene into geometry-regularized 3D Gaussian Splats.
paper_summary.summary: WorldMesh introduces a method for generating navigable multi-room 3D scenes from text prompts by first constructing an explicit 3D mesh scaffold for geometric structure and then conditioning image diffusion models on this scaffold for photorealistic appearance. This approach yields significantly improved 3D consistency and quality for large-scale environments, with users preferring WorldMesh in 96.2% of comparisons against baselines.

title: WorldMesh: Generating Navigable Multi-Room 3D Scenes via Mesh-Conditioned Image Diffusion

abstract:
Recent advancements in diffusion models have significantly enhanced the data
synthesis with 2D control. Yet, precise 3D control in street view generation,
crucial for 3D perception tasks, remains elusive. Specifically, utilizing
Bird's-Eye View (BEV) as the primary condition often leads to challenges in
geometry control (e.g., height), affecting the representation of object shapes,
occlusion patterns, and road surface elevations, all of which are essential to
perception data synthesis, especially for 3D object detection tasks. In this
paper, we introduce MagicDrive, a novel street view generation framework,
offering diverse 3D geometry controls including camera poses, road maps, and 3D
bounding boxes, together with textual descriptions, achieved through tailored
encoding strategies. Besides, our design incorporates a cross-view attention
module, ensuring consistency across multiple camera views. With MagicDrive, we
achieve high-fidelity street-view image & video synthesis that captures nuanced
3D geometry and various scene descriptions, enhancing tasks like BEV
segmentation and 3D object detection.

organization_info.name: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

keyInsights: Explicitly encoding diverse 3D geometric information (camera poses, 3D bounding boxes, road maps) rather than relying on 2D projections enables precise 3D control and preserves crucial depth and height cues.

An implicit view transformation from 2D BEV maps to 3D camera views can be effectively learned within the diffusion model by synergizing with injected scene-level camera pose and 3D object-level embeddings.

A local cross-view attention mechanism that attends to immediate neighboring views is sufficient and effective for maintaining multi-camera consistency in sparsely arranged camera setups typical of autonomous driving.
originalProblem: Autonomous driving systems face high costs and logistical challenges in collecting and annotating real-world data for training robust deep learning models.

Existing generative models primarily rely on 2D layouts, lacking the precise 3D geometric control (object heights, road elevations, occlusion patterns) and multi-camera view consistency essential for realistic synthetic data in autonomous driving.
Prior methods for street view generation with 3D geometry often lose critical 3D information during 3D-to-2D projection or struggle with consistency across sparse camera views.
results: MAGICDRIVE significantly reduced the FID score to 16.20 (224x400), outperforming BEVGen (25.54) and BEVControl (24.85), indicating superior image realism and fidelity.

Synthetic data generated by MAGICDRIVE effectively augmented training, boosting BEV segmentation (Vehicle mIoU +4.34%, Road mIoU +5.26%) and 3D object detection (mAP +2.52 for CAM-only) for state-of-the-art perception models.

The framework demonstrated fine-grained control at scene-level (weather, time), background-level (road maps), and foreground-level (object manipulation), along with consistent multi-camera video generation.
solution: A novel framework is developed, fine-tuning a pre-trained Stable Diffusion model and incorporating separated encoding strategies for diverse 3D geometric conditions at scene, foreground, and background levels.

Scene-level conditions (text, camera pose), foreground (3D bounding boxes), and background (2D BEV road maps) are encoded distinctly and injected into the UNet, with camera pose and 3D bounding boxes utilizing cross-attention, and road maps an additive encoder.

A cross-view attention module is introduced to aggregate information from immediate neighboring camera views, ensuring coherence and consistency across multiple sparse camera perspectives in the generated scenes.
paper_summary.summary: MAGICDRIVE introduces a framework for multi-camera street view generation with precise 3D geometry control, leveraging separated encoding strategies for diverse conditions and a cross-view attention module. It achieves high-fidelity image and video synthesis and significantly improves downstream 3D perception tasks like BEV segmentation and 3D object detection through data augmentation.

title: MagicDrive: Street View Generation with Diverse 3D Geometry Control

abstract: We present DriveGen3D, a novel framework for generating high-quality and highly controllable dynamic 3D driving scenes that addresses critical limitations in existing methodologies. Current approaches to driving scene synthesis either suffer from prohibitive computational demands for extended temporal generation, focus exclusively on prolonged video synthesis without 3D representation, or restrict themselves to static single-scene reconstruction. Our work bridges this methodological gap by integrating accelerated long-term video generation with large-scale dynamic scene reconstruction through multimodal conditional control. DriveGen3D introduces a unified pipeline consisting of two specialized components: FastDrive-DiT, an efficient video diffusion transformer for high-resolution, temporally coherent video synthesis under text and Bird's-Eye-View (BEV) layout guidance; and FastRecon3D, a feed-forward module that rapidly builds 3D Gaussian representations across time, ensuring spatial-temporal consistency. DriveGen3D enable the generation of long driving videos (up to $800\times424$ at $12$ FPS) and corresponding 3D scenes, achieving state-of-the-art results while maintaining efficiency.
title: DriveGen3D: Boosting Feed-Forward Driving Scene Generation with Efficient Video Diffusion

abstract: Text-to-3D generation has achieved remarkable success via large-scale text-to-image diffusion models. Nevertheless, there is no paradigm for scaling up the methodology to urban scale. Urban scenes, characterized by numerous elements, intricate arrangement relationships, and vast scale, present a formidable barrier to the interpretability of ambiguous textual descriptions for effective model optimization. In this work, we surmount the limitations by introducing a compositional 3D layout representation into text-to-3D paradigm, serving as an additional prior. It comprises a set of semantic primitives with simple geometric structures and explicit arrangement relationships, complementing textual descriptions and enabling steerable generation. Upon this, we propose two modifications -- (1) We introduce Layout-Guided Variational Score Distillation to address model optimization inadequacies. It conditions the score distillation sampling process with geometric and semantic constraints of 3D layouts. (2) To handle the unbounded nature of urban scenes, we represent 3D scene with a Scalable Hash Grid structure, incrementally adapting to the growing scale of urban scenes. Extensive experiments substantiate the capability of our framework to scale text-to-3D generation to large-scale urban scenes that cover over 1000m driving distance for the first time. We also present various scene editing demonstrations, showing the powers of steerable urban scene generation. Website: this https URL.

organization_info.name: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

title: Urban Architect: Steerable 3D Urban Scene Generation with Layout Prior

abstract: Synthesizing extrapolated views remains a difficult task, especially in urban driving scenes, where the only reliable sources of data are limited RGB captures and sparse LiDAR points. To address this problem, we present PointmapDiff, a framework for novel view synthesis that utilizes pre-trained 2D diffusion models. Our method leverages point maps (i.e., rasterized 3D scene coordinates) as a conditioning signal, capturing geometric and photometric priors from the reference images to guide the image generation process. With the proposed reference attention layers and ControlNet for point map features, PointmapDiff can generate accurate and consistent results across varying viewpoints while respecting geometric fidelity. Experiments on real-life driving data demonstrate that our method achieves high-quality generation with flexibility over point map conditioning signals (e.g., dense depth map or even sparse LiDAR points) and can be used to distill to 3D representations such as 3D Gaussian Splatting for improving view extrapolation.
title: Pointmap-Conditioned Diffusion for Consistent Novel View Synthesis

abstract: Perpetual 3D scene generation aims to produce long-range and coherent 3D view sequences, which is applicable for long-term video synthesis and 3D scene reconstruction. Existing methods follow a "navigate-and-imagine" fashion and rely on outpainting for successive view expansion. However, the generated view sequences suffer from semantic drift issue derived from the accumulated deviation of the outpainting module. To tackle this challenge, we propose ScenePainter, a new framework for semantically consistent 3D scene generation, which aligns the outpainter's scene-specific prior with the comprehension of the current scene. To be specific, we introduce a hierarchical graph structure dubbed SceneConceptGraph to construct relations among multi-level scene concepts, which directs the outpainter for consistent novel views and can be dynamically refined to enhance diversity. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework overcomes the semantic drift issue and generates more consistent and immersive 3D view sequences. Project Page: this https URL.

keyInsights: Encoding multi-level scene concepts and their intricate relations into a hierarchical graph structure (SceneConceptGraph) is crucial for maintaining semantic consistency across diverse 3D generations.
Combining Textual Inversion and DreamBooth for scene-specific prior customization allows diffusion models to deeply understand and preserve scene identity, preventing semantic drift.
Dynamically refining the concept graph and leveraging test-time training provides a mechanism for controllable scene evolution and real-time editing while preserving overall coherence.
originalProblem: Existing "navigate-and-imagine" methods for perpetual 3D scene generation suffer from semantic drift, where scene identity degrades over long sequences.
Methods that avoid semantic drift often produce monotonous or content-limited scenes, lacking visual diversity.
Scene-level customization, involving complex relations among multiple concepts, is inadequately addressed by current object-centric customization techniques.
results: ScenePainter achieved superior scene-level customization, with DINO scores of 0.931 and CLIP-I scores of 0.952, outperforming baselines in fidelity.

User studies for 3D views generation showed ScenePainter was strongly preferred for visual quality (89.3% over WonderJourney), diversity (83.4% over WonderJourney), and consistency (92.6% over WonderJourney).
Ablation studies confirmed the necessity of the hierarchical SceneConceptGraph and its loss components (L_rec, L_prior, L_attn) for maintaining quality, diversity, and coherent object distributions.
solution: A two-stage framework is proposed, starting with Concept Relation Construction to build a scene-specific prior from a single image using a hierarchical SceneConceptGraph.
The SceneConceptGraph captures multi-level concepts (overall environment, category regions, individual objects) and their interrelations through learned textual embeddings.
Concept Relation Refinement dynamically adjusts the SceneConceptGraph during generation, enabling interactive editing and ensuring semantic consistency while generating new content.
paper_summary.summary: ScenePainter introduces a framework for perpetual 3D scene generation that resolves the semantic drift issue by aligning an outpainter's scene-specific prior with a hierarchical SceneConceptGraph and dynamic refinement. This method generates semantically consistent and visually diverse 3D view sequences, with user studies showing strong preference over previous methods for visual quality, diversity, and consistency.

title: ScenePainter: Semantically Consistent Perpetual 3D Scene Generation with Concept Relation Alignment

abstract: Recent advances in driving-scene generation and reconstruction have demonstrated significant potential for enhancing autonomous driving systems by producing scalable and controllable training data. Existing generation methods primarily focus on synthesizing diverse and high-fidelity driving videos; however, due to limited 3D consistency and sparse viewpoint coverage, they struggle to support convenient and high-quality novel-view synthesis (NVS). Conversely, recent 3D/4D reconstruction approaches have significantly improved NVS for real-world driving scenes, yet inherently lack generative capabilities. To overcome this dilemma between scene generation and reconstruction, we propose WorldSplat, a novel feed-forward framework for 4D driving-scene generation. Our approach effectively generates consistent multi-track videos through two key steps: (i) We introduce a 4D-aware latent diffusion model integrating multi-modal information to produce pixel-aligned 4D Gaussians in a feed-forward manner. (ii) Subsequently, we refine the novel view videos rendered from these Gaussians using a enhanced video diffusion model. Extensive experiments conducted on benchmark datasets demonstrate that WorldSplat effectively generates high-fidelity, temporally and spatially consistent multi-track novel view driving videos. Project: this https URL

keyInsights: Directly generating an explicit 4D Gaussian representation from multi-modal latents allows for both generative flexibility and geometrically consistent novel-view synthesis.
A feed-forward architecture for 4D scene generation enables scalability and avoids the computational cost of per-scene optimization common in reconstruction methods.
Decomposition into static and dynamic Gaussians, aggregated in a unified ego-coordinate system, is crucial for maintaining spatio-temporal coherence across multi-frame scenes.
originalProblem: Traditional driving-scene generation models excel in 2D video realism but often lack 3D consistency and capability for novel-view synthesis.
Urban scene reconstruction methods, while providing strong 3D/4D consistency, are fundamentally reconstructive and cannot generate novel scenarios or diverse conditions.
A critical dilemma exists in autonomous driving simulation, where a single framework capable of both generative imagination and faithful 4D reconstruction is needed.
results: Achieved best scores for original view video generation, with an FVD_multi of 74.13 and FID_multi of 8.78 without first-frame guidance, outperforming existing baselines.
Demonstrated superior novel-view synthesis, consistently recording the best FID and FVD scores across various viewpoint shifts (e.g., FID 8.25, FVD 40.17 at ±1m shift, outperforming DiST-4D).
Improved downstream autonomous driving tasks, achieving 38.49% mIoU and 29.34% mAP for BEV perception, and boosting the StreamPETR mAP by 4.0% when combined with real data.
solution: WorldSplat proposes a feed-forward framework that directly generates 4D driving scenes without per-scene optimization.

It integrates a 4D-aware latent diffusion model to synthesize multi-modal latents (RGB, depth, dynamic object masks) from various control conditions (BEV layout, instance boxes, ego trajectory, text).
These latents are decoded into an explicit 4D Gaussian representation, which is then used to render novel-track videos, subsequently refined by an enhanced diffusion model for improved realism.
paper_summary.summary: WorldSplat, developed by Xiaomi EV and academic partners, introduces a framework for generating dynamic 4D driving scenes. It unifies generative capabilities with 3D consistency by producing an explicit 4D Gaussian representation, enabling high-fidelity multi-track videos and novel views that are spatio-temporally consistent.

title: WorldSplat: Gaussian-Centric Feed-Forward 4D Scene Generation for Autonomous Driving

abstract: Diffusion models are advancing autonomous driving by enabling realistic data synthesis, predictive end-to-end planning, and closed-loop simulation, with a primary focus on temporally consistent generation. However, large-scale 3D scene generation requiring spatial coherence remains underexplored.
